name: Avoid Encodings
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 2: Meaningful Names
problem: Type or scope information encoded into identifier names via Hungarian Notation, m_ prefixes, or I-prefixed interfaces adds cognitive overhead, becomes stale when types change, and clutters modern IDE-aided development.
steps: Remove type prefixes from variable names (e.g., rename strName to name, iCount to count).
Remove m_ or _ member-variable prefixes; keep classes and methods small enough to make scope obvious.
Leave interfaces unadorned; if encoding is needed, suffix the implementation instead (ShapeFactoryImpl, not IShapeFactory).
Rely on the IDE and strong type system rather than name-embedded type hints.
Review legacy code and incrementally strip obsolete encoding prefixes.
